description: Brand voice forbids over-apologetic phrases. Customer complains about a delayed order; agent must reply without banned apology phrases (objective substring gate, no LLM judge).

orgSettings.brandVoice: Confident and direct. Never over-apologize. Do NOT use the phrases 'so sorry' or 'deeply apologize' — they sound performative. Acknowledge the issue briefly and move straight to what's happening and what's next.

messages.contentText: My order #2002 is taking forever to arrive. This is really frustrating, when will it actually ship?

instruction: Reply to the customer about their delayed order.

checks.description: Reply avoids over-apologizing as instructed by the brand voice in orgSettings ('Confident and direct. Never over-apologize.'). At most one brief acknowledgement of the inconvenience; no stacked or performative apology phrasing beyond what the banned substring list already catches.
